summary.intent: Enforce a consistent 12-character minimum password length across the entire codebase, matching the backend's validation in auth_routes.rs.
summary.outcome: All 16 occurrences across 9 files were updated and pushed. A follow-up formatting fix was required after rustfmt (nightly) reformatted long assert_eq! lines in sandbox tests.

repo: Password minimum length is enforced in auth_routes.rs (backend) and must be mirrored in: locale strings (en/fr), client-side JS validation, Rust FFI bridge (ffi_auth.rs), macOS Swift app (AppSettings.swift), and docs.
The project uses a pinned nightly rustfmt, which can reformat long assert_eq! macro calls differently than stable, causing format-check CI failures after pushes.

code.finding: Long assert_eq! calls must be formatted with rustfmt nightly's multi-line style; single-line form fails format-check.
Same multi-line assert_eq! formatting requirement applies here.

workflow: CI runs zizmor on GitHub Actions workflows; 28 findings are suppressed, and the release workflow passed clean.
Local checks run in parallel (fmt, biome, i18n, install-names, install-docs, file-size); a single failure aborts the suite with 'One or more parallel local checks failed.'
The project enforces a 1500-line file size limit with an allowlist for exceptions.

friction: Nightly rustfmt reformatted two assert_eq! lines in sandbox tests after the main push, requiring a separate fix commit — the format-check failure only surfaced post-push.
